title: Breeding NONcNZO10/LtJ and NON/ShiLtJ Mice
author: Ian Simpson
license_note: Confirmed via CrossRef DOI metadata: dx.doi.org/10.17504/protocols.io.556g89e

steps.title: Mice are bred at 6 to...
steps.instruction: Mice are bred at 6 to 7 weeks of age or later. Set up two females with one male.

steps.title: Every 6 months new male and...
steps.instruction: Every 6 months new male and females breeders are purchased from JAX laboratories.

steps.title: The mice are set up for 5 days
steps.instruction: The mice are set up for 5 days. Typically the mice are set up on a Thursday and the male is separated on the Tuesday and the females remain together.

steps.title: The Friday before the females are...
steps.instruction: The Friday before the females are due to have pups, the cages are changed, and provided with fresh water and the food hopper is filled. Check to see which females are pregnant. Then place a “Do Not Disturb” sign on the cage.

steps.title: For the week that the females...
steps.instruction: For the week that the females start having pups, try not to move the cages in and out when trying to check for litters. Just try looking in the cages to see if you see any litters.

steps.title: After the pups are born, wait...
steps.instruction: After the pups are born, wait a week and then remove the do not disturb sign. Fill water bottle and food hoppers if needed.

steps.title: Cages that have two litters in...
steps.instruction: Cages that have two litters in same cage, check the sex of the pups at 2 to 2.5 weeks of age. Then divide the male pups evenly between both cages. If you have an odd number of males, place the extra male in one of the cages (he will be kept as a breeder). Then divide the females to even out both litters, giving both dams the same number of pups.

steps.title: Wean the mice at 4 weeks...
steps.instruction: Wean the mice at 4 weeks of age (+/- 1 day from the date of birth). Wean the males in pairs. Any odd number males from litters will be kept as breeders. Females can be housed together, with up to 5 females per cage.

steps.title: At weaning, the experimental NON/NZ &...
steps.instruction: At weaning, the experimental NON/NZ & NON/Shi mice are placed on autoclaved for 20 min. at 120 C, 5K20 (Animals Specialties and Provisions, LLC ) diet. All breeder mice will be kept on 2016 (Harlan Laboratories diet).

steps.title: Each experimental mouse will be ear tagged
steps.instruction: Each experimental mouse will be ear tagged. The lowest ear tag number will be in the left ear of the one mouse and the highest ear tag number in the right ear of the other mouse. Ear tag the 1 st mouse and then weigh the mouse. Place the mouse into the new cage. Then ear tag the 2 nd mouse, then weigh and place into the new cage with 1 st mouse ear tagged. Get a new cage and repeat ear tagging and weighing the next pair of mice.

steps.title: Also wean the female mice at this time
steps.instruction: Also wean the female mice at this time. The females are all placed on the 2916 diet and do not need weighed or blood glucose. The male breeders are also placed on the 2916 diet and do not need weighed or blood glucose.

steps.title: Mice are weighed every other week...
steps.instruction: Mice are weighed every other week for the first four weeks on the 5K20 diet.

steps.title: Then the mice are weighed and...
steps.instruction: Then the mice are weighed and blood glucose levels are checked at 8 weeks old and then checked every other week after thereafter and measurements are taken up to 14 weeks on 5K20 Diet. At 6 weeks on 5K20 diet, a nestlet is placed into the experimental mice cages and the nesting sheets removed.

steps.title: Mice should be weighed and blood...
steps.instruction: Mice should be weighed and blood glucose levels measured before 10:00am.

steps.title: Mice are moved on Thursday’s, usually...
steps.instruction: Mice are moved on Thursday’s, usually at 19 weeks of age to CG740H in CAQ for experimentation

steps.title: Mice that have blood glucose levels...
steps.instruction: Mice that have blood glucose levels 250mg/dL or over are considered diabetic and glucose levels below 250mg/dL are controls. See Figure 1
